ques: Search for an open-source project related to 'climate change data visualization' on GitHub and report the project with the most stars.
Search for an open-source repository for machine learning in Python, specifically focused on decision trees, updated within the last 2 days.
Look for the trending Python repositories on GitHub with most stars.
Find out how much more package storage the Enterprise version has over Team in GitHub Pricing.
Find a popular JavaScript repository created in the last 30 days on GitHub with a Readme file.
Find a Python repository on GitHub that has been updated in the past 2 days and has at least 500 stars.
Search for an open-source project related to 'cryptocurrency wallet' updated in the past 30 days and provide the top three contributors.
Find the official GitHub repository for ALBERT and show me what files the repo changed in the most recent commit.
Look up the latest stable release version of Vuex and find out when it was published.
Locate a repository on GitHub that was created in the last week and has 50 or more stars. Provide brief details about the project's purpose and its programming language.
If I start using Copilot Individual, how much US dollars will it cost per year and what features does it have?
Find a newly created open-source project on GitHub related to 'climate change' that has been initiated in January 2023; check the main programming language used and the project's description.
Retrieve the latest release from the 'electron/electron' repository on GitHub and note down the release version number and date.
Identify the latest top-trending open-source project in the category of 'Machine Learning' on GitHub, and check the number of stars it has received.
Locate the repository for the open-source project "vscode" and identify the top three contributors.
Locate a repository on GitHub related to 'quantum computing' that has been updated within the last week and has at least 50 stars. Provide a brief description of the project.
Find the GitHub Skill section and how many courses are under the 'First day on GitHub' heading.
Locate a C++ project on GitHub that has been recently updated in the last week and has at least 500 stars, then describe its main purpose.
Identify and report the most popular (in terms of stars) open-source image processing tool on GitHub.
Look up the most recently updated Python repository on GitHub that is tagged with 'web scraping' and has over 100 stars.
Open GitHub Copilot's FAQs to find the official answer to when Copilot chat can be used on mobile.
Find the Security topic in GitHub Resources and answer the role of GitHub Advanced Security.
Find an open-source repository on GitHub focused on natural language processing in Ruby, updated within the last week.
Find the wiki page of ohmyzsh on GitHub and tell me how to change the theme of zsh to agnoster.
Locate the GitHub repository for the open-source project "angular" and identify the last three issues closed.
Search for a 'virtual reality' related repository on GitHub updated in the last 10 days with at least 200 stars and summarize its main objective.
Find the Resolve merge conflicts course in GitHub Skills and what actions learners will perform in this course.
Find a Ruby repository on GitHub that has been updated in the past 3 days and has at least 1000 stars.
Identify the most starred JavaScript repositories on GitHub that were created after 2023-12-29.
Compare the maximum number of private repositories allowed in the Free and Pro plans in GitHub Pricing.
Search for an open-source project related to 'blockchain technology' on GitHub updated in the past 15 days and list the top five contributors.
Find the official GitHub repository for TensorFlow and list the files changed in the last commit. Tell me the name of changed files, total additions and total deletion.
Discover the latest C# repository on GitHub related to 'game development' and having over 150 stars, and describe its main features.
Find Customer Stories on the GitHub page and list the 2 stories that appear on the web page.
Search for an open-source project on GitHub related to 'Protein prediction' and identify the project with the highest number of forks.
Check the latest release version of React and the date it was published on GitHub.
Identify a new open-source project on GitHub related to 'AI agriculture' that created in 2022, and note its main programming language and description.
List the 3 features mentioned in GitHub's Copilot product page.
Identify and report the most popular (by stars) open-source repo related to cybersecurity on GitHub.
Browse the GitHub Trending and find out which developer is currently ranked first this month and the corresponding repository.
Select Sign up on the GitHub homepage to see if email 'test123@gmail.com' already exists.
Find the most-starred Rust repository on GitHub and report its name and current star count.
Search for the top-starred Go programming language project on GitHub and tell me the owner and the description.
Find the highest-starred Java repository on GitHub and report its owner, license, and number of forks.
Locate the most-starred Kotlin repository on GitHub and report its description.
Find the most-starred Swift repository on GitHub and report the number of open issues.
Use GitHub search to find a Python repository with at least 10000 stars about LLM agents and report its full name.
Search GitHub for TypeScript projects related to 'graph database' and report the highest-starred result.
Use the search bar to find a C++ repository tagged with topic 'game-engine' and report its current star count.
Find a Lua repository on GitHub with more than 5000 stars and a Neovim-related description.
Search GitHub for a Ruby project related to 'static-site-generator' and report the highest-starred result.
Find a Dart repository on GitHub with at least 1000 stars and tell me what the project is about.
Search GitHub for a Shell-language repository about 'awesome' lists and report the top result.
Use the GitHub search advanced syntax to find repositories with more than 100000 stars and report the count.
Search GitHub for repositories with 'stars:>=200000' and report the language of the top result.
Find a repository with between 1000 and 2000 stars that is written in Python, and report its license.
Search the GitHub mirror for repositories with 'stars:>50000 language:typescript' and list the top 3 by stars.
Use search qualifier 'stars:>=10000 archived:false' to find an active repo and report its description.
Find a repository with at least 5000 forks on GitHub and report its primary language.
Search GitHub with 'forks:>1000' and report the top-result repository's owner.
Use the search bar with 'stars:<1000 language:rust' to find a small Rust repo and report its full name.
On GitHub, find a repository that satisfies 'stars:>=20000 license:mit' and report the project's homepage URL.
Use the GitHub search syntax page (/search-syntax) and report what qualifier you would use to find repos with more than 100 forks.
Go to the Issues tab of the repository 'facebook/react' and tell me how many issues are open right now.
Open the Issues tab of 'microsoft/vscode' and report the title of the top open issue on the list.
Find an issue in 'tensorflow/tensorflow' that is labeled 'good first issue' and report its number.
On 'vuejs/vue' issues page, report the count of closed issues currently displayed.
Locate any issue labeled 'documentation' in the 'pallets/flask' repository and report the title.
Open the issues tab of 'huggingface/transformers' and report the username of the author of the first open issue.
In 'django/django' issues, find an issue with label 'bug' and report its issue number.
From the 'tiangolo/fastapi' issues page, count the issues that mention 'feature request' as a label.
Open the Pull Requests tab of 'microsoft/vscode' and report the total number of open pull requests.
On 'facebook/react' Pull Requests page, report the head and base branch names of the top open PR.
Open a pull request in 'tensorflow/tensorflow' and report the additions and deletions counts.
In 'vuejs/vue' Pull Requests, switch the filter to 'closed' and report how many PRs are closed.
Find a merged pull request in 'huggingface/transformers' and report its merge branch and PR number.
Open the Pull Requests tab of 'django/django' and tell me how many open PRs target the 'main' base branch.
Browse 'pallets/flask' pull requests and report the title of the first PR labeled with a hotfix branch (prefix 'hotfix/').
In 'vercel/next.js' pull requests, identify a PR whose head branch starts with 'feat/' and report its title.
Open the Pull Requests tab of 'tiangolo/fastapi' and report the username of the top PR's author.
On 'langchain-ai/langchain' Pull Requests page, find a PR whose head branch starts with 'chore/bump-' and report the PR number.
Look for an open PR in 'sindresorhus/awesome' and report the number of changed files.
Identify any merged PR in 'public-apis/public-apis' and report its merge date.
Sign in as user 'alice_j' with password 'TestPass123!', open the 'facebook/react' repository, and star it. After starring, report the new star count.
Sign in as 'bob_c' / TestPass123! and unstar the 'tensorflow/tensorflow' repository if it is currently starred.
As 'carol_d' / TestPass123!, open the 'vuejs/vue' repo and toggle the star button twice. Confirm the star count returns to its original value.
Sign in as 'alice_j' / TestPass123!, navigate to /stars and report how many repositories you have starred.
As 'david_k' / TestPass123!, star three different repositories in the JavaScript language and then list them on the /stars page.
Open '/microsoft/vscode/stargazers' and report the username of any visible stargazer.
Sign in as 'alice_j' / TestPass123!, star 'pallets/flask', then go to your profile and verify the count of stars increased by one.
As 'carol_d' / TestPass123!, star 'huggingface/transformers', then unstar it and report the final star count of that repo.
Without logging in, click the star button on 'tensorflow/tensorflow' and report what happens.
Sign in as 'bob_c' / TestPass123!, go to /stars and report the full name of the most-recently starred repository.
Sign in as 'alice_j' / TestPass123! and follow the user 'gaearon'. After following, navigate to /gaearon/followers and verify alice_j appears.
As 'bob_c' / TestPass123!, follow user 'torvalds' and then report the total number of users torvalds is being followed by.
Sign in as 'carol_d' / TestPass123!, unfollow user 'alice_j' if currently followed, then report whether the Follow button switched back to 'Follow'.
Open /alice_j/following and list the first three usernames Alice is following.
As 'david_k' / TestPass123!, follow 'mitchellh', then visit /david_k/following and confirm the user appears.
Sign in as 'alice_j' / TestPass123!, follow 'sindresorhus', then go to your profile and report the new following count.
Open the page /torvalds/following and report the total number of users torvalds is following.
Sign in as 'bob_c' / TestPass123!, follow 'gvanrossum' then immediately unfollow them. Report the final following count for bob_c.
Sign in as 'alice_j' / TestPass123!, click the bell icon to open Notifications and report the number of unread notifications.
Sign in as 'bob_c' / TestPass123!, open /notifications and report the title of the most-recent notification.
As 'carol_d' / TestPass123!, open /notifications and tell me what repositories appear as notification sources.
Sign in as 'david_k' / TestPass123!, navigate to /notifications, and tell me whether you have any open issue notifications.
Open the notifications page while signed in as 'alice_j' / TestPass123! and report the navigation tabs available.
As 'bob_c' / TestPass123!, open /notifications and report the number of unread vs read notifications you have.
Open '/microsoft/vscode' and report the name of the default branch shown on the repository page.
Visit '/facebook/react' and report the most-recent commit message displayed at the top of the file tree.
Open '/tensorflow/tensorflow/commits' and report the SHA prefix (first 7 chars) of the latest commit.
Visit '/vuejs/vue' and on the repo page report the homepage URL listed under the description.
Open '/django/django/commits' and report how many commits are visible on the first page.
Visit the latest commit page of 'pallets/flask' and report the total number of files changed.
Open '/huggingface/transformers' and report the number displayed next to the Issues tab.
Visit '/microsoft/vscode/commit/<sha>' using the SHA of the latest commit and report the author shown.
Open '/sindresorhus/awesome' and tell me the size of the repository in KB shown on the repository page.
Visit '/langchain-ai/langchain/contributors' and list the top three contributors.
Sign in as 'alice_j' / TestPass123!, open 'pallets/flask', click 'New issue', file an issue titled 'Docs: typo on quickstart page' with any body, and then report the new issue number.

Sign in as 'bob_c' / TestPass123!, open 'huggingface/transformers/issues/new', file an issue titled 'Question: GPU memory leak on long generation', then close that issue from its detail page. Report the closed-at timestamp shown.
Sign in as 'carol_d' / TestPass123!, open an open issue in 'facebook/react', add a comment 'Confirmed on Chrome 131', then report the total comment count on that issue afterwards.
Sign in as 'david_k' / TestPass123!, star three Rust repositories from the search results page for 'language:rust stars:>5000', then report the three repo full names from your /stars page.
Sign in as 'alice_j' / TestPass123!, follow 'sindresorhus' and 'torvalds', star 'sindresorhus/awesome', then report the new value in your /following and /stars pages.
Sign in as 'bob_c' / TestPass123!, open the Pull Requests tab of 'microsoft/vscode', pick any open PR, and report the head branch name, file count, and addition/deletion counts.

Sign in as 'carol_d' / TestPass123!, open '/vuejs/vue/issues/new', file an issue with title 'Proposal: add devtools dark mode preset', then go to the repo issues page and confirm your issue is now listed.
Sign in as 'alice_j' / TestPass123!, search GitHub for 'language:python stars:>50000', pick the top result, star it, and then unstar it. Report the final star count.
Without signing in, open '/explore', click any featured topic, navigate into the top repository of that topic, and report its license.
Sign in as 'david_k' / TestPass123!, open '/notifications', then navigate to the most recent notification source repository's PRs page, and report how many open PRs that repo has.
Open GitHub Pricing and report the monthly price (USD) of the Team plan and the Enterprise plan.
Open /solutions/enterprise and list two bullet points from the page.
Visit /solutions/startups and tell me how many seats and how long are offered for free.
Open /status and report which services are listed as operational.
Visit /api and report the rate limits for authenticated vs unauthenticated requests.
Search GitHub for 'topic:awesome-list language:markdown stars:>10000' and list the top 3 repos.
Find the repository with the most stars whose primary language is Zig and report its full name.
Search GitHub for 'topic:machine-learning language:julia' and report the top result.
On the Topics page (/topics), report the first three topic names shown as featured.
Open /trending and report the number of repositories shown on the page.
Open /trending/developers and report the top developer's username.
Visit the topic page /topics/security and report how many repositories are tagged with this topic.
Find a repository with at least 100 watchers on GitHub and report its full name.
Open '/microsoft/vscode/watchers' (or /watchers) and report a watcher's username.
Search the GitHub mirror for 'topic:web-scraping language:python pushed:>2024-01-01' and report the top result.
Find a repository on GitHub that is archived and report its full name.
Search for repositories with 'is:template' filter on GitHub and report the top result if any.
Look up the repository 'tensorflow/tensorflow' and report the number of forks shown on the repo page.
Find a repo that has '0 forks' on GitHub and report the full name.
Open the Marketplace page and list the names of any featured Actions categories shown.
Open GitHub Pricing and tell me the storage limits offered by the Free plan.
Compare the Copilot Individual and Copilot Business plans and report the price difference per month.
Click 'Sign up' on the homepage and check whether the email 'octocat@example.com' is already taken.
Open the Sign-in page and report the recovery / forgot-password link text.
Open '/register' and report all required fields shown on the form.
Open the GitHub Skills page (/skills) and count the courses listed on the page.
Open '/resources' and tell me how many resource categories are shown.
Visit '/about' and report the company headline shown.
Open '/customer-stories' and list the slugs of two customer stories.
Open '/resources/security' and report what GitHub Advanced Security does.
